title: 7 Password Registration and Login Process
body_text:
7	Password Registration and Login Process
7.1	Overview of Password Registration and Login Process
Client Centre Users and CPS Admin Security Officers log in to the Client Centre to register their user IDs and passwords. CPS Admin Security Officers are responsible for verifying the Client Centre Users芒聙聶 identity before allowing them to proceed with registration.
A CPS Admin Security Officer must have a 2-step security token assigned to their profile in order to log in to CPS Admin. See the User Management Part for procedures on assigning tokens.
Client Centre Users only require a 2-step security token to access an application (or functionality within an application) that requires step-up authentication (i.e., Wires).
Important
:	Any changes made to a Client Centre User芒聙聶s profile on CPS Admin (e.g., password reset) may take up to 15 minutes to come into effect on the Client Centre.
7.2	Client Centre Password Registration (All Users)
This section describes how to register a Client Centre password and applies to both CPS Admin Security Officers and Client Centre Users. Follow these procedures when logging in for the first time or after a password reset. Password registration is complete once the user ID is entered and the one-time password is changed. User IDs and one-time passwords are obtained as follows.
Client Centre User
芒聙聯 Obtain the user ID and one-time password from a CPS Admin Security Officer.
CPS Admin Security Officer
芒聙聯 Central聽1 communicates the one-time password directly to the CPS Admin Security Officer. The CPS Admin Security Officer芒聙聶s user ID is recorded on the CPS Admin Appointment Certificate, Form 1918.
To register your password:
Go to the
https://clients.central1.com
.
Enter your username and one-time password and then select
Sign in
. See Figure 1.
Figure 1: Logging in to the Client Centre
Enter your old password (the one-time password) when prompted and then enter and confirm a new password. Select
Submit
. See Figure 2.
The new password must be a minimum of eight characters and can contain alphanumeric and special characters, including capital letters. It can芒聙聶t be the same password used as one of the last six passwords.
Passwords are checked against a list of known insecure passwords to prevent the use of passwords that are known to have been exposed or shared from internet data breaches. This means that some password changes will fail even though the new password meets the structure requirements. If this occurs, the user should select a new password or use a passphrase instead, as these are harder to guess (e.g., 芒聙聹deskchairphonemug芒聙聺 or 芒聙聹I LOVE ice Cream day芒聙聺).
Figure 2: Password Reset
Related Information
CPS Admin Appointment Certificate,
Form 1918
7.3	Logging in to the Client Centre (All Users)
This section applies to Client Centre Users and CPS Admin Security Officers.
To log in to the Client Centre: Go to
https://clients.central1.com
. Enter your username and password, then select
Sign in
. See Figure 3.
Note: 	You芒聙聶ll be prompted to enter your username before this page appears if it芒聙聶s your first login to the Client Centre, you芒聙聶ve deleted your cookies, or you芒聙聶re using incognito/private browsing.
Figure 3: Client Centre Login
Select
Applications
. See Figure 4.
Figure 4: Accessing Applications on the Client Centre
Select
the application you want to access
. See Figure 5.
Note: 	Applications are grouped by type (e.g., Admin & Support, Payments). If needed, scroll down the page to find the application. If you芒聙聶ve saved the application as a favourite, it will also appear under 芒聙聹My Favourites芒聙聺.
Figure 5: Production Applications Page
Follow the prompts if 2-step security authentication is required to access an application.
7.4	Logging in to CPS Admin
This section applies to CPS Admin Security Officers only. CPS Admin Security Officers must log in to CPS Admin with a 2-step security token. See the 2-Step Security Part for information on ordering hard tokens and installing and registering soft tokens. While Central聽1 generally assigns the token to new CPS Admin Security Officers during set-up, CPS Admins Security Officers can assign tokens to other CPS Admin Security Officers via the User Management application. See the User Management Part for procedures on assigning 2-step security tokens.
To access CPS Admin:
Go to
https://clients.central1.com
.
Enter your username and password and then select
Sign in
. See Figure 6.
Figure 6: Client Centre Login
Select
Applications
. See Figure 7.
Figure 7: Accessing Applications on the Client Centre
Afterwards, select
CPS Admin
. See Figure 8.
Figure 8: Accessing CPS Admin
Enter the code that is generated on your registered 2-step security token. Select
Done
. See Figure聽9.
Figure 9: Entering the Code from the 2-Step Security Token
Select
Click here
. See Figure 10.
Figure 10: Select Click Here
Note: 	Always log out of CPS Admin and close the browser before allowing another user to access CPS Admin from the same computer. There is a 30-minute inactivity time-out period on CPS Admin. If login problems occur, see Chapter 8, Troubleshooting CPS Admin Problems.
END
